name: WAVM
summary: WebAssembly virtual machine that compiles every Wasm module via the full LLVM optimisation pipeline. Maximises peak performance for compute-heavy long-running workloads at the cost of multi-second per-module compile times. Useful when the Wasm module is a stable, long-lived computation kernel.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: WAVM is a WebAssembly runtime that takes the opposite trade-off from Wasmtime's Cranelift backend or Wasmer's multi-backend design: every Wasm module goes through the full LLVM optimisation pipeline. The result is peak code quality for any given Wasm input, at the cost of multi-second compilation per module.

sections.title: Use cases
sections.content:
WAVM is most useful when the Wasm module is a long-running computation kernel where peak throughput matters more than startup latency. Scientific computing, simulation engines, ML inference engines, and similar long-lived workloads benefit from spending compile time once at startup in exchange for steady-state performance close to native code.

For short-lived workloads (edge functions, plug-ins loaded on demand, sandboxed user scripts), WAVM's compile-time cost is unaffordable. Cranelift-based runtimes (Wasmtime, Wasmer with Cranelift) or even Wasmer's SinglePass are better fits there.
